content_markdown: What is **not** an assumption of the kinetic model of an ideal gas?

options.label: A
options.text: Attractive forces between molecules are negligible.

options.label: B
options.text: Collision duration is negligible compared with time between collisions.

options.label: C
options.text: Molecules suffer negligible momentum change during wall collisions.

options.label: D
options.text: Molecular volume is negligible compared with gas volume.

vibe_explanation: The kinetic model of an ideal gas assumes that collisions with the walls are perfectly elastic. This means that while kinetic energy is conserved, the momentum of a molecule changes from $+mv$ to $-mv$ (a total change of $2mv$). If the momentum change were negligible, the gas would exert no pressure on the container walls, which contradicts the fundamental definition of gas pressure ($P = F/A = \frac{\Delta p / \Delta t}{A}$).
